title: Obligation Breadth Expansion
decision_question: Which additional follow-through surfaces should extend obligation modeling so the tool can answer what else must change with higher precision?
hypothesis: DTOs, config keys, registries, command and status surfaces, public APIs, sibling clone families, and explicit validation surfaces will materially improve missing-followthrough guidance if they are tied to changed symbols and concrete dependent surfaces.

variants.name: Current contract triggers
variants.description: Current closed-domain and contract-driven obligation detection.

variants.name: DTO, config, registry expansion
variants.description: Add follow-through modeling for DTOs, config keys, registries, and command/status surfaces.
variants.policy_delta: Expand obligation graph breadth and dependent surface hints.

variants.name: API, clone, test, docs expansion
variants.description: Add sibling clone family propagation, public API dependents, and concrete validation and documentation surfaces.
variants.policy_delta: Extend changed-symbol precision and stale validation surface detection.

exit_bar: Move beyond a generic something is missing judgment and point to concrete dependent surfaces and likely fix sites.
Reject expansions that increase reviewed precision noise or broad stale-surface guessing.
Promote only the obligation families that improve repair-packet completeness and remediation success.
